sections.label: Lyrics
lines: The air was heavy with rust and stone
Chains like cold hands gripped the bone
But silence broke in sacred thread
Two voices rising where hope had fled
Walls don't speak, but they can listen
To midnight hymns of the unforgiven
Praise, like water, filled the cell
A whisper lit the gates of hell
We sang 'til the river ran through the dark
'Til the ground split open with a burning spark
Even stone remembers the sound of grace
When chains fall quiet, in their place
We sing, we sing—
The river runs midnight through everything
No torch, no sword, no earthly key
But still the doors swung wild and free
Not for escape, but for belief
That heaven blooms beneath our grief
The deepest pit can hold a choir
If every tear becomes a fire
If silence dares to clap its hands
Then angels dance on prison lands
We sang 'til the river ran through the dark
'Til the ground split open with a burning spark
Even stone remembers the sound of grace
When chains fall quiet, in their place
We sing, we sing—
The river runs midnight through everything
So let the dark be not the end
But the place where praises bend
And if you listen close tonight...
You’ll hear the river, running bright
